instruction:
For each problem, select the best response.
(a) The alternative hypothesis for an ANOVA F test is that [ANS] A. at least two of the population means are not equal.  B. none of the population variances are equal.  C. all of the population variances are not equal.  D. all of the population means are not equal.  E. none of the population means are equal.
(b) The null hypothesis for an ANOVA F test is that [ANS] A. population means are equal.  B. sample means are equal.  C. sample standard deviations are not equal.  D. population standard deviations are not equal.  E. sample sizes are equal.
(c) In contrast to a chi-square test, an ANOVA F test is most appropriate when the [ANS] A. standard deviations must be estimated.  B. data are quantitative.  C. number of samples is two.  D. samples sizes are equal.  E. standard deviations are not equal.
